headline: Malaysia's Productivity Growth Flatlines at 1.2% — Lowest in 20 Years
context: MPC reported that Malaysia's labour productivity growth stalled at 1.2% in 2025, the lowest in two decades, as the economy remained trapped in a middle-income model dependent on low-wage labour rather than innovation-driven output.

cards.big: 1.2% productivity growth — Malaysia is stuck in a middle-income trap where workers work harder but the economy doesn't get smarter
cards.sub: Vietnam's productivity grew 5.8% and South Korea's 3.2% — Malaysia's 1.2% means falling further behind every year

cards.big: GDP per worker at RM98,000 is 62% below South Korea's RM258,000 and 78% below Singapore's RM445,000
cards.sub: Low productivity means low wages — the two are mathematically linked and no wage policy can override productivity reality

cards.big: 82% of Malaysian workers are in low-skill or semi-skilled roles — highest proportion among upper-middle-income ASEAN nations
cards.sub: Only 28% of workforce holds tertiary qualifications versus South Korea's 52% and Singapore's 48%

cards.big: R&D spending at 1.04% of GDP is half the 2% target and one-third of South Korea's 4.8%
cards.sub: Government's National Productivity Blueprint identified 42 reform actions — only 18% were implemented after 3 years

cards.big: Productivity is the only sustainable path to higher wages — everything else is a temporary fix for a permanent problem
Should Malaysia triple R&D tax incentives to 300% and mandate that GLCs spend minimum 3% of revenue on innovation?
